title: Buy'ce gal, buy'ce tal
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1|notoc=1}} {{Dablink|For the other Mandalorian drinking song, see ''[[Naasad'guur mhi – Mhi n'ulu]]''}} {{InUniverseMedia |image= |name=''Buy'ce gal, buy'ce tal'' |author= |editor= |illustrator= |artist= |performer=[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]]{{InsiderCite|86|The Mandalorians: People and Culture}} |publisher= |release date= |media type= |pages= |published in= |genre=[[Alcohol/Legends|Drinking]] [[Music/Legends|song]] }} '''''Buy'ce gal, buy'ce tal''''' was a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Alcohol/Legends|drinking]] [[Music/Legends|song]]. By the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], ''Buy'ce gal, buy'ce tal'' had gained significant popularity among numerous Mandalorian [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]].''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]'' The song was cynical in nature, and was an expression of the common Mandalorian [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary's]] wry pragmatism as they accepted that life could be fleeting and victories irrelevant. Traditionally sung in the Mandalorian [[Language/Legends|dialect]] of [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']], ''Buy'ce gal, buy'ce tal'' was a song that contained strong language. When it was performed in the more widely spoken language of [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], ''Buy'ce gal, buy'ce tal'' was often given an approximate translation rather than a direct one, with the lyrics edited to be less explicit.
{|{{Prettytable}} !''Mando'a''||Phonetic pronunciation||Approximate Basic||Direct BasicAs can be concluded by reading [[Karen Traviss]]'s [https://web.archive.org/web/20130617082501/http://www.karentraviss.com:80/page20/page26 notes and vocabulary list]. |- | colspan="1" |''Buy'ce gal, buy'ce tal
Verbor'ad ures aliit
Mhi draar baat'i meg'parjii'se
Kote lo'shebs'ul narit.''|| colspan="1" |BOO-shay GAHL, BOO-shay TAHL
vair-BOR-ahd OO-rehs AH-leet
Mee DRAHR bah-TEE mayg-PAR-jee-SAY
koh-TAY loh SHEBS-ool-NAH-reet|| colspan="1" |A pint of ale, a pint of blood
Buys men without a name.
We never care who wins the war
So you can keep your fame. || colspan="1" |A pint of ale, a pint of blood
Hires a man without identity.
We never care who the winners are
So shove your glory up your ass. |}
==Behind the scenes== First appearing in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[canon]] in the opening of the twenty-first chapter of ''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]'' by [[Karen Traviss]], the song was identified only as a nameless popular Mandalorian drinking chant. With the release of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article, ''[[The Mandalorians: People and Culture]]'', in the magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 86|eighty-sixth issue]], the song was officially deemed "''Buy'ce gal, buy'ce tal''" and a brief interpretation of its lyrical meaning was provided. In the novel ''Triple Zero'', the translation that is given in Basic is labeled as an "approximate" one, edited for strong language. ==Appearances== *''[[Republic Commando: Triple Zero]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|86|The Mandalorians: People and Culture}} {{1stID|''Buy'ce gal, buy'ce tal''}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Cántico de Bebida Mandaloriana }} [[Category:Mandalorian chants and songs]]
